title: Garner's Modern English Usage
excerpt: The most authoritative and comprehensive guide to modern English usage, combining descriptive accuracy with prescriptive guidance, featuring Garner's trademark Language-Change Index.
keyIdeas: Usage is not static — language evolves, and good guidance must recognize this
The Language-Change Index provides a rational framework for evaluating disputed usage
Prescriptive and descriptive approaches can be reconciled through evidence-based judgment
keyTakeaways: Language change is inevitable but not all changes are equally acceptable
The Language-Change Index rates disputed usages from Stage 1 (rejected) to Stage 5 (universally accepted)
Good usage balances logic, historical precedent, and current practice
Context determines what is appropriate — formal and informal usage differ legitimately
Writers should master the standard before choosing to depart from it
whoShouldRead: Students of English language and linguistics
Non-native speakers seeking authoritative usage guidance
Anyone who cares about precise, effective English usage
whoShouldSkip: Casual readers looking for a quick style guide
Those who find 1000+ page reference books intimidating
Readers who want only prescriptive rules without nuance
